Malosi  

Description

2: Decrease Cold Resist by 11 (L48) to 70 (L51)
3: Decrease Magic Resist by 11 (L48) to 70 (L51)
4: Decrease Poison Resist by 11 (L48) to 70 (L51)
5: Decrease Fire Resist by 11 (L48) to 70 (L51)

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 175 Skill: Alteration
Casting Time: 5 Recast Time: 6
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Resist: Magic
Resist Adjust: -10 Range: 200
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Deletable: Yes Dot Stacking: Yes
Reflectable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Single
Spell Type: Detrimental Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You feel very vulnerable.
Cast on other: Soandso looks very uncomfortable.
Effect Fades: Your vulnerability fades.

Game Description

Decreases your target's resistance to cold, fire, magic, and poison.

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Malosi

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Malosi
ClassLevel
SHM48
MAG51
Expansion:

Duration:
10.6 mins @L48 to 14.0 mins @L65
  • Comments (11)
  • Sold By (11)
  • NPCs with Ability (24)
  • Screenshots
Post Comment
this works
QuoteReply
# Nov 02 2008 at 3:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1602461961302
104 posts
i"M fighting mobs in the caves in W L none of my spells stuck to them used this then my spells worked ,i use this first to pull them
▲ Page top
Dispell?
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2008 at 7:08 PM Rating: Decent
Drazzminius
Scholar
188 posts
I get Mobs in TBS and such casting this at me all the time. Will cancel magic negate it? I'm thinking so, just haven't tried it yet...
____________________________
Mikaal Drazzminius
Tier'Dal Necromancer
Necrotalk Admin
EQ Traders Super Moderator
EQ Magelo Advisor/Moderator
Rodcet Nife by Birth / Quellious by Merger / Povar by Re-Merger
▲ Page top
resist
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2006 at 6:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591898863617
15 posts
Hey Killabear, you're right, slow has a zero 0 magic resist check, it has a disease resist check. If you dont beleive me, slow yourself and then cast remove disease and see if it doesnt remove it. Cast this first.
▲ Page top
resist
QuoteReply
# Nov 23 2006 at 10:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592288262087
54 posts
Actually you can have a spell that is a check on one resist, but a counter on another. And yes shammies have both slows that are checked on magic and slows that are checked on disease, the best of which are checked on magic.
▲ Page top
malosi
QuoteReply
# Jul 14 2006 at 4:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759790599
1 post
This spell can be purchased at the common spell vendor in Abysmal Sea named Godain Mackodor.
▲ Page top
slow
QuoteReply
# Apr 17 2004 at 2:13 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591791021110
152 posts
mage cast this asap, it doesnt have high agro (can easily pull with it), so if it does hit before slow it should help slow land.
▲ Page top
Casting this spell prior to slow
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2004 at 5:07 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787816227
118 posts
I honestly don't see the point of it most times. Both the lvl 51 slow spell and this one have a magic resist check of 0, which means either the slow or this spell are equally likely to land. so instead of casting this spell first, you might as well take your chances with slow (which you usually need asap) and then cast malo second.
▲ Page top
Casting this spell prior to slow
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2008 at 2:19 PM Rating: Decent
Rickymaru
Scholar
490 posts
Things aren't the way they were back in 2004 or 2005. Trash mobs can easily hit for upwards of 3k a hit. Slow needs to land asap if you want to keep the tank alive. Named mobs in SoF expansion can easily hit for more then 3k and very fast too. Level 80 some what gimpy monk was getting hit for a little over 6k at max hit by a squire in crystallos. Those are single groupable. If your a shaman and have the aa slow try it right away. If it doesn't work then try to use your favorite debuff and retry but otherwise always cast it first.
▲ Page top
RE: Casting this spell prior to slow
QuoteReply
# Dec 29 2005 at 5:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591821849628
4 posts
sorry just read this post and just dont agree with what u are saying about it dont matter which you cast first whether it be a slow or a malosi. I have played most classes, malosi is best to get on immediatly. Slow effects to classes, cleric and the tank, while the malo will effect the entire party to be able to land higher dam there for saving the cleric mana becuase mob dies faster. and will allow for the slow to land alot easier. just my opinion though
▲ Page top
RE: Casting this spell prior to slow
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2004 at 12:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592258027798
78 posts
This spell is one of a mages most usefull spells when soloing.. it saves a greeat deal of mana
